creator: bkelly@vadev.org
text:
I am running RELENG_6 from around June 13, 2007.  See the uname -a 
output I included in the bug report for the exact version.

I believe the problem is not that @cwd is unrecognized, but that it is 
placed on a separate line from its argument.  Once I switched the "echo 
@cwd" to an "echo -n @cwd" everything worked fine.  Are plist entries 
supposed to work split across lines?

Thanks.
